rationale: The citing paper is a methodological investigation of key-informant accuracy, explicitly modeling method variance (MTMM CFA) and offering guidance on survey design/triangulation, while the cited paper is a methodological study on cross-sectional vs. longitudinal surveys, CMV, and design guidelines in marketing surveys. Given this strong method-level overlap (despite only one DOI-matched reference entry and no in-text snippet), the citation could be a substantive methods/foundational source and should not be dropped at triage.
